title: eriogonum heermannii var. subspinosum
intro: Eriogonum heermannii var. subspinosum, commonly known as Heermann's buckwheat or California buckwheat, is a shrub in the buckwheat family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum heermannii var. subspinosum include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
